title: Not Great, Not Terrible
description: Experience your first core meltdown.

title: Unplanned Disassembly
description: Have a component explode due to over-pressurization.

title: Thermal Runaway
description: Reach criticality (200% heat) without any Vents on the grid.

title: Riding the Redline
description: Remain in repulsion (heat > 110% of max) with hull integrity above 0% for 60 consecutive ticks.

title: Critical Margin
description: Maintain reactor heat above 95% of max capacity for 120 consecutive ticks without melting down.

title: Controlled Burn
description: Maintain 100% heat capacity for 10 consecutive ticks while generating at least 500,000 Power.

title: Chain Reaction
description: Have exactly 10 components explode simultaneously on the grid in a single tick.

title: Thermal Whiplash
description: Recover from criticality (heat > 150%) back to nominal (heat < 80%) without manually clicking 'Reduce Heat'.

title: Measure Twice
description: Draft a layout in the Blueprint Planner with Net Heat ≤ 0 and > 0 Power.

title: Thermodynamic Equilibrium
description: Achieve a Net Heat of exactly 0 while generating >5,000 Power.

title: Closed Loop
description: Have a stable reactor (Net Heat ≤ 0) running with 0 Vents.

title: Unstable Equilibrium
description: Generate >5,000,000 Power per tick with exactly 0 Vents placed.

title: Sub-Compact Core
description: Generate >100,000 Power per tick on a grid of 36 tiles or fewer (6x6 size).

title: Cryogenic Fission
description: Generate >10,000 Power per tick while maintaining current reactor heat at exactly 0.

title: Thermal Capture Program
description: Generate at least 1,000 Power per tick exclusively from Stirling Generator vent upgrades.

title: Waste Heat Reclamation
description: Have a layout where your Stirling Generators produce more power than your primary fuel cells.

title: Stakhanovite Plan
description: Produce >1,000,000 Power with a total grid value under $50,000.

title: Pressure Cooker
description: Have all active Heat Exchangers on the grid at exactly 100% heat capacity simultaneously.

title: The Cold Shoulder
description: Have 4 active Heat Inlets drawing heat from a single central cell.

title: The Straitjacket
description: Place a single fuel cell surrounded by 4 check valves creating a perfect heat-locking loop.

title: Singularity Horizon
description: Place 4 Black Hole Particle Accelerators on the grid simultaneously.

title: Closed-Door Research
description: Establish your first Experimental Laboratory (purchase Level 1).

title: Protium Breeder Program
description: Have 10 active Protium Cells on the grid simultaneously.

title: Full Spectrum Reflection
description: Place 12 Thermal Neutron Reflectors on the grid simultaneously.

title: Thermionic Harvest
description: Have 15 active Thermionic Coolant Cells on the grid simultaneously.

title: Vortex Exchange
description: Have both an Extreme Heat Inlet and an Extreme Heat Outlet active on the grid.

title: Supercritical Vortex
description: Have 8 active Extreme Vents on the grid simultaneously.

title: The Protium Era
description: Generate >50,000,000 Power per tick using only Protium Cells as your power source.

title: Catalytic Synthesis
description: Reach Level 10 of the Sub-Atomic Catalysts upgrade.

title: Hawking Evaporation
description: Have a Black Hole Particle Accelerator reach at least 99% of its maximum heat capacity.

title: Unified Field Theory
description: Unlock all 9 experimental component types in your research tree.

title: Nuclear Disarmament
description: Perform an Exotic Particle Weave (Prestige) with exactly 1 active fuel cell on your grid.

title: The Perfect Weave
description: Complete an Exotic Particle Weave with session power produced and session heat dissipated within 0.1% of each other.

title: Sympathetic Resonance
description: Have a layout where a single central Reflector tile is adjacent to 4 active, fully enriched Quad Plutonium Cells.

title: Frozen Fire
description: Have a Quad Nefastium Cell running at 100% output while its local heat is kept at exactly 0.
